Ophiopogon japonicus, also known as Ophiopogon, is a type of perennial plant native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ophiopogon japonicus include oligosaccharides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ophiopogon japonicus is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is prepared in various herbal preparations, including tea, capsules, and tinctures, as well as being used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
